source: Hand-curated from MLB.com, Baseball Hall of Fame, Baseball Reference franchise pages, and Cardinals team history records
note: Rosters of Hall of Famers and retired numbers are standing team records. Historic seasons are summaries, not comprehensive game recaps.

retiredNumbers.note: HOF 2B, later manager and coach for ~70 years with the organization
HOF 1969 · 3-time MVP · .331 career BA · 475 HR · 3,630 hits
HOF outfielder · 'Mad Dash' in 1946 World Series
HOF 1953 · 1934 NL MVP · last NL 30-game winner
HOF closer · split-finger fastball pioneer (also MLB-wide retirement for Jackie Robinson)
HOF 1981 · 1968 NL MVP/Cy Young · 1.12 ERA in 1968 · Game 1 WS-record 17 K vs Tigers
Team owner · number equals his age when honored

hallOfFamers.name: Rogers Hornsby
hallOfFamers.note: Career .358 BA, second only to Cobb. Two Triple Crowns.

hallOfFamers.name: Grover Cleveland Alexander
hallOfFamers.note: Game 7 strikeout of Tony Lazzeri sealed 1926 WS.

hallOfFamers.name: Frankie Frisch
hallOfFamers.note: Player-manager of the 1934 Gas House Gang.

hallOfFamers.name: Enos Slaughter
hallOfFamers.note: 'Mad Dash' to score winning run in Game 7 of 1946 WS.

hallOfFamers.name: Bob Gibson
hallOfFamers.note: 1968 MVP/Cy Young. 1.12 ERA that year led to the mound being lowered in 1969.

hallOfFamers.name: Lou Brock
hallOfFamers.note: Traded from Cubs for Ernie Broglio — one of the most lopsided trades in MLB history.

hallOfFamers.name: Steve Carlton
hallOfFamers.note: Won four Cy Youngs after leaving for Philadelphia.

hallOfFamers.name: Ozzie Smith
hallOfFamers.note: Perhaps the greatest defensive shortstop ever. 'Go Crazy, Folks!' HR in 1985 NLCS.

hallOfFamers.name: Bruce Sutter
hallOfFamers.note: Won 1982 WS Game 7, throwing the final pitch.

hallOfFamers.name: Whitey Herzog
hallOfFamers.note: Architect of Whiteyball — speed, defense, pitching.

hallOfFamers.name: Tony La Russa
hallOfFamers.note: Two World Series titles (2006, 2011). 3rd all-time in wins as manager.

historicSeasons.headline: First championship
historicSeasons.description: Rogers Hornsby player-manager. Grover Cleveland Alexander — picked up mid-season — struck out Tony Lazzeri in Game 7 with the bases loaded to preserve the title.

historicSeasons.headline: The Gas House Gang
historicSeasons.description: Dizzy Dean 30-7, brother Paul 19-11. Beat Detroit in 7. Joe Medwick ejected from Game 7 for his own safety after being pelted with fruit by Tigers fans.

historicSeasons.headline: Musial era begins
historicSeasons.description: Stan Musial's first full season. Cardinals won 106 games and beat the Yankees in 5 — New York's first WS loss since 1926.

historicSeasons.headline: Streetcar Series
historicSeasons.description: Cardinals beat the St. Louis Browns in the only all-St. Louis World Series, played entirely at Sportsman's Park.

historicSeasons.headline: The Mad Dash
historicSeasons.description: Enos Slaughter scored from first on a single in the 8th of Game 7 against the Red Sox, winning the title. Musial's first postseason MVP-caliber season.

historicSeasons.headline: Gibson arrives
historicSeasons.description: Philadelphia's late-season collapse handed the Cardinals the pennant. Bob Gibson won Games 5 and 7 over the Yankees. Ken Boyer won NL MVP.

historicSeasons.headline: El Birdos
historicSeasons.description: Lou Brock set the WS record for steals (7). Gibson won three games in the Series over Boston.

historicSeasons.headline: Year of the Pitcher
historicSeasons.description: Gibson's 1.12 ERA and 13 shutouts. Lost Game 7 to Mickey Lolich and the Tigers. The mound was lowered for 1969 partly in response to this season.

historicSeasons.headline: Whiteyball
historicSeasons.description: Whitey Herzog's first title. Bruce Sutter threw the final pitch of Game 7 against Milwaukee. Ozzie Smith's first season in St. Louis.

historicSeasons.headline: The Denkinger Call
historicSeasons.description: Ozzie Smith's 'Go Crazy, Folks!' walk-off HR clinched NLCS vs Dodgers. Lost WS to Royals after a controversial call by 1B umpire Don Denkinger in Game 6.

historicSeasons.headline: Pujols era peak
historicSeasons.description: Beat Detroit in 5. Cardinals won 83 regular-season games — fewest for any WS champion since 1888.

historicSeasons.headline: The Rally Squirrel
historicSeasons.description: 10.5 games back in late August, Cardinals made the playoffs on the final day. Came within one strike of losing twice in Game 6 vs Texas. Won Game 7 the next night.

traditions.name: The Rally Squirrel
traditions.description: During Game 4 of the 2011 NLDS at Busch Stadium, a squirrel ran across home plate during a pitch. It returned for Game 5 and became the unofficial mascot of the 2011 championship run.

traditions.name: Clydesdales
traditions.description: The Anheuser-Busch Clydesdales circle the warning track on Opening Day and for special occasions — a tradition dating to 1933 when August Busch Sr. used Clydesdales to deliver the first Budweiser after Prohibition's end.

traditions.name: Two birds on the bat
traditions.description: The classic Cardinals logo — two cardinals perched on a baseball bat — was inspired by Allie May Schmidt, who saw cardinals at a church picnic in 1921 and sketched the image. Used ever since.
